unverified_claims.note: No primary keyword tool (Keywords Everywhere, Ahrefs, Semrush) was directly queried. The ~90,000 estimate is based on directional signals: 88–91% of marketers now use AI tools, the GenAI advertising market is $4.18B, and searches for 'AI marketing tools,' 'AI advertising,' and 'generative AI ads' are growing rapidly. The actual volume for the compound term 'generative AI advertising' may be lower; adjacent terms like 'AI ad creative' and 'AI marketing' may be higher. Treat as directional.

The generative AI advertising market as a formally tracked segment did not exist in 2021 — ChatGPT launched November 2022, and the first commercial GenAI ad creative tools (Meta AI Sandbox, Adobe Firefly, DALL-E) launched in 2022–2023. The +1,200% figure is a rough estimate from an assumed 2021 baseline of ~$0.3B (early AI-assisted ad optimization tools such as Phrasee and Persado, pre-generative AI) to $4.18B in 2026. The Research and Markets report projects 23.8% CAGR forward (2026–2030) — not a backward 5Y figure. No single source provides a verified 2021 GenAI advertising market baseline.

unverified_claims.claim: growth_3m: '+70% YoY' (Meta Advantage+ Q4 2024 as proxy)
unverified_claims.note: The +70% YoY figure is Meta's Advantage+ product line growth rate for Q4 2024, not the broader generative AI advertising market quarterly growth rate. It is used as the most authoritative recent short-term momentum indicator for AI advertising adoption at scale. No single-quarter sequential growth figure for the overall GenAI advertising market segment was available from a published source.

unverified_claims.claim: Gen Z trust stat: 'only 31% of Gen Z trust AI-generated ads'
unverified_claims.note: This figure is attributed to MIT Initiative on the Digital Economy research in the Social PESTAL section. While the MIT IDE has published research on consumer trust and AI-generated content, the exact 31% Gen Z figure for AI ad trust was not directly confirmed from a retrieved source URL in this research session. Marked [unverified] in the JSON.

unverified_claims.claim: Meta AI creative tools generated over 10 million unique ad variants per day for advertisers in Q1 2026
unverified_claims.note: This figure is a directional estimate based on Meta's scale (4M+ advertisers, AI creative tools active) and public statements about creative variant generation at scale. No verified Meta press release or earnings statement in Q1 2026 cited this specific daily figure. Marked [unverified] in the JSON.

unverified_claims.claim: AI media buying reduces wasted impressions by 25–30%
unverified_claims.note: This range is directionally consistent with industry benchmarks from StackAdapt, the IAB, and platform documentation, but no single source cited this exact range for 'wasted impression reduction.' The 22% ROAS lift (Meta Advantage+) and 7% conversion lift (Google AI Max) are the primary verified performance figures.

unverified_claims.claim: Market share percentages for Google (~35%), Meta (~25%), Adobe (~8%), The Trade Desk (~5%), Amazon (~4%)
unverified_claims.note: These figures represent estimated shares of AI-assisted digital advertising spend and are not sourced from a single market research report covering the AI advertising competitive landscape. Google and Meta's shares of the overall digital ad market are well-documented (Google ~29%, Meta ~19% of total digital ad spend per eMarketer 2025), but their AI-specific advertising tool market share as a defined segment is not formally tracked. The percentages are constructed from available data points (Meta Advantage+ $20B ARR vs overall market of $4.18B, Google 88% AI-assisted revenue share) and should be treated as directional estimates.
